Fremantle’s AI-driven Imaginae Studios preps “quality-first cultural project” as its debut
Fremantle’s AI-driven label Imaginae Studios is prepping its first project, the short-form series Art Awakens, which utilizes generative AI to explore some of the world’s most famous and iconic paintings.
The ...
By
Justin Anderson
April 9, 2026
Fremantle’s AI-driven label Imaginae Studios is prepping its first project, the short-form series
Art Awakens
, which utilizes generative AI to explore some of the world’s most famous and iconic paintings.
The series, which a release says will remain “rooted in artistic craft and cultural integrity,” reimagines masterpieces across five centuries, from Edvard Munch’s
The Scream
(1893) to Jan van Eyck’s
The Arnolfini Portrait
(1434) to Vincent van Gogh’s
The Starry Night
(1889), with each piece “transformed into a cinematic moment” that will bring audiences inside the world of that painting by giving voice and movement to the images.
Fremantle launched the AI-native Imaginae Studios
a year ago
to capitalize on the rapidly evolving technology.
Art Awakens
was created in collaboration with award-winning Spanish AI filmmaker Hilario Abad, who is recognized internationally for his work in AI cinema.
Imaginae says the project was designed as a “quality-first cultural project” rather than a tech demonstration and is intended to reflect the new label’s ambition to build an IP slate of “genuine creative merit” while pioneering generative AI technology as a creative medium through AI-native production.
The announcement adds that
Art Awakens
is representative of the content that Imaginae Studios is developing, designed for global audiences and with potential for partnerships with broadcasters, publishers and various online platforms.
“
Art Awakens
marks an important first project for the studio,” said Fremantle Group COO and CEO for Europe Andrea Scrosati in a statement. “From the outset, our ambition has been to give new creative talent the space to experiment and the freedom to realize bold ideas using incredible AI tools. I believe this series is a perfect demonstration of that in action. It shows how generative AI, guided by creative instinct and craft, can unlock new forms of storytelling and create meaningful, culturally resonant content for global audiences.”
Further details on the rollout and distribution of
Art Awakens
will be announced in the coming months.
(
Image: Imaginae Studios
)

About The Author
Justin Anderson joined
Realscreen
as senior staff writer in 2021, reporting and writing stories for the newsletter and magazine. During his 20-year career he’s filled a variety of roles as a writer and editor at a number of media organizations, covering news and current affairs as well as business, tech, the film and music industries and plenty in between. He’s also spent time behind the scenes in television production, having written everything from voiceover scripts for documentaries to marketing copy. He has a degree in Journalism from Toronto Metropolitan University (formerly Ryerson University).
